question.text: The Code of Conduct for RTAs is prescribed under which schedule of the SEBI (RTA) Regulations?

answer.text: Schedule IV with 20 points of conduct
Schedule III with 31 points of conduct
Schedule I with 15 points of conduct
Schedule II with 25 points of conduct

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Code of Conduct for RTAs is specified in Schedule III of the SEBI (Registrars to an Issue and Share Transfer Agents) Regulations, 1993, and contains 31 specific points of professional conduct. **The Trap:** The schedule number (III) and the count (31 points) are both specific. Exam questions may test either or both specifics. **Cross-Exam Context:** Every SEBI intermediary has a Code of Conduct. The RTA's 31-point code is comprehensive, covering professional standards, confidentiality, conflict of interest, and operational integrity. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Code of Conduct ensures your RTA maintains professional standards. If you ever face service quality issues with an RTA, the Code of Conduct provides the benchmark against which complaints can be filed.
